summary: A Revision Reminder App with spaced repetition scheduling and study growth tracking. Users can add topics, which get scheduled for review at 1-day, 3-day, 7-day, 1-month, and 3-month intervals. The app tracks revision history and displays a cumulative study growth chart.
architectural_notes: Study progress tracked as a list of (timestamp, count) tuples for cumulative charting
Spaced repetition intervals updated to: 1-day, 3-day, 7-day, 1-month, 3-month; each interval label shown alongside topic name in due reminders UI

existing_features.title: Build a frontend page that allows users to add new topics via a text input and submit button. Display the full list of added topics.

Build a frontend section that displays all currently due reminders (topics whose scheduled revision interval has been reached). Each due reminder shows the topic name and interval label (e.g., '1-day', '3-day', '30-day', '3-month'). Users can click a 'Mark as Revised' button on each due reminder to complete it.

Build a frontend study growth chart that visualizes cumulative topics revised over time. The X-axis represents time (revision session timestamps) and the Y-axis represents the cumulative total number of topics revised. Use a line chart with data points from getStudyProgress.

Apply a clean, focused study-app visual theme: use a warm parchment/cream background with deep forest green and amber accent colors, a readable serif or semi-serif heading font paired with a clean sans-serif body font, and a calm card-based layout with subtle shadows. Apply this theme consistently across all pages and components.

Update the DueReminders frontend component to display the due date (or time remaining) alongside each reminder's interval badge. For each due reminder item, show a human-readable label indicating when it is due (e.g., 'Due today', 'Due in 2 days', 'Overdue by 1 day'). Also update the interval badge labels to match the new set: '1-day', '3-day', '7-day', '1-month', '3-month'.

Update the TopicManager component and any other frontend section that lists or references reminder intervals to display all five intervals (1-day, 3-day, 7-day, 1-month, 3-month) with their scheduled due dates visible. If the topic list shows upcoming reminder dates per topic, ensure all five interval entries are rendered with a formatted date string.

new_feature_requests.summary: Add topics to a revision list with spaced repetition reminders (1-day, 3-day, 30-day, 3-month)
Check and trigger due reminders periodically, marking them as completed
Display a cumulative study growth chart showing total topics revised over time

Create a backend data model and actor in Motoko that stores topics, their spaced repetition reminder schedules (1-day, 3-day, 30-day, 3-month intervals from creation time), and a study progress log (timestamp + count of topics revised). Expose the following API: addTopic(name: Text), getTopics(), getDueReminders(), markReminderDone(topicName: Text, interval: Text), getStudyProgress().

Update the backend spaced repetition intervals from the current set (1-day, 3-day, 30-day, 3-month) to (1-day, 3-day, 7-day, 1-month, 3-month). Update all interval label strings, scheduling logic, and any hardcoded interval arrays in backend/main.mo to reflect the five intervals: '1-day', '3-day', '7-day', '1-month', '3-month'.
